description:
Slime Rancher 2 — Archipelago multiworld randomizer.

As Beatrix LeBeau you explore the Far, Far Range, collecting slimes and
opening new zones.  In AP mode, treasure pods, gordos, map nodes, drones
and fabricator upgrades become location checks, and your vacpack upgrades
arrive as items from across the multiworld.

v0.2.0.description:
Initial fixes for the Slime Rancher 2 v1.2 release - Radiant Slime Sanctuary

No new content in this release, just getting the mod working with the new game version. Updates to include the new features will be in a later update
v0.2.0.title: v0.2.0

v0.2.1.description:
Adds Slimepedia support for Radiant Slimes, save file fixes, and also fixes the Settings UI bug with the Archipelago icon.

Also includes a generated write-up of how radiant slime spawn behavior works
v0.2.1.title: v0.2.1

v0.2.2.description:
* Updates regional logic for slimepedia entries
* Adds option to modify chance for radiant slimes
* Fixes more reconnecting bugs
* Fixes region unlock settings and edge cases
* Adds in initial/basic support for plort door randomization (logic still needed)
* Misc logic/formatting for the debug menu
v0.2.2.title: v0.2.2

v0.3.0.description:
* Adds 2 new weather-related options to the implementation
  * Modifier for weather change rates
  * Adds the ability to only have level 3 weather to guarantee weather-related slimes
* New "Always Radiant" option for slimes
* Reclassifies the "Slime Ring" item from `trap` to `filler`
v0.3.0.title: v0.3.0

v0.3.1.description:
Features
* Adds a new setting, `radiant_spawn_rate_multiplier`, which allows the player to increase the rate at which radiant slimes spawn without making all slimes radiant

Bug fixes
* Ephemeral items (filler/traps) no longer replay on reconnect if they were already applied in the past
* Updated Teleport Trap handling to ensure players don't end up in a location they shouldn't be in yet (Grey Labyrinth)
v0.3.1.title: v0.3.1

v0.3.2.description:
Features
* Adds new setting to increase spawn rates of Gold/Lucky Slimes

Bug fixes
* Fixes crashing problem with shadow plort dispensers
v0.3.2.title: v0.3.2

v0.3.3.description:
Bug fixes:
* Save and Quit from within game now successfully disconnects the player from the Archipelago server
* Teleport traps can now only send players to zones they've physically visited, not just ones they have unlocked

Behavior change:
* When `region_access_mode` is set to `locations` or `bundled`, the portal switches are now no longer automatically pressed - The player must still interact with the buttons/doors after being granted access to open the ruins/doors

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Rhelys/SlimeRancher2-AP/compare/v0.3.2...v0.3.3
v0.3.3.title: v0.3.3

v0.3.4.description:
New Feature:
* `DisableTarr` setting now in the apworld - forcibly disables the Tarr Slimepedia check and the 2 Tarr-related traps regardless of other settings in the yaml if enabled.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Rhelys/SlimeRancher2-AP/compare/v0.3.3...v0.3.4
v0.3.4.title: v0.3.4

v0.4.0.description:
Logic Updates
* Fixes some of the previously wrong area logic for resources
* Adds jetpack requirements to relevant checks in the Conservatory and Rainbow Fields

Quality of Life
* Set friendly names for location checks in the Conservatory and Rainbow Fields

Bug Fixes
* Fixes the bug where the Fabricator would provide the upgrade in the slot and the location check
v0.4.0.title: v0.4.0

v0.4.1.description:
Bug fixes
* Fixes the issue with items/upgrades being replayed on reconnect - you should be able to save/quit now and reload without losing upgrades.
* Fixes traps being replayed too quickly when loading into the game and crashing - there's now a time delay between the game finishing loading and traps being applied
v0.4.1.title: v0.4.1

v0.4.2.description:
Bug Fixes
* Now returns the player to spawn if they return to a zone when the right door isn't opened

This happened when a portal was used to a zone where the region access hasn't been triggered yet (field buttons for EV/SS, Puzzle Door for PB) and then the player tried to come back through the normal portal, essentially softlocking them.
v0.4.2.title: v0.4.2

v0.4.3.description:
Bug Fixes:
* Fixes the Trap item delay blocking other items from being granted
* New attempt at fixing the load from save bug replaying all items

QoL Improvements:
* Adds partially complete friendly names for Ember Valley treasure pods
v0.4.3.title: v0.4.3

v0.5.0.description:
Experimental support for the new SR2 patch versions 1.2.2 and 1.2.3. The mod builds successfully and no longer crashes, however there are still some things not yet tested with the changes

- Plort doors
- Gordos being popped
- Goal conditions
- Upgrade items

Also a known issue with the wrong items being sent after a full `Save/quit > Exit game > Open game > Continue` cycle. You will want to play this implementation in one sitting for now until the next update
v0.5.0.title: v0.5.0 (Experimental)

v0.5.1.description:
Still labeling this release as experimental for the new SR2 patches as not all the features have been fully vetted post-update yet.

Bug Fixes
- Items should be received normally after a save/quit/continue loop
v0.5.1.title: v0.5.1 (Experimental)

v0.5.2.description:
New Additions
* Option added to start with the Resource Harvester rather than having players need to set it as a starting item

_Not having the Resource Harvester locks you out of most Fabricator checks and quite a few Slimepedia checks for a gadget that's typically the game's first expected upgrade. Having this now available as a setting will give players the option to speed up the early game check options a bit._

Balance Adjustments
* Tarr Rain trap now lasts 60 seconds (down from 180 seconds)

_Tarr Rain can be really oppressive even for shorter periods of time. The duration may be dropped even further (30-45 seconds) after additional testing/feedback._

Other Adjustments
* Reworked the trap queuing system
  * Traps now respect zone loading and pause until the player has fully loaded in
  * Queue timing before: Traps were all queued and fired with a 30 second delay between traps
  * Queue timing after: 3 separate trap queues depending on the type of trap, each with their own delay timer
    * Spawner traps (Tarr/Slime Ring): 1 second delay
    * Teleport traps: 5 second delay 
    * Weather traps: 30 second delay (unchanged)

_It felt weird for traps to release over a long period of time when someone releases a large number of items, so this should hopefully fix some of that experience and clear out the incoming traps more quickly._
v0.5.2.title: v0.5.2

v0.6.0.description:
## Note: This release is backwards incompatible with previous versions (including the apworld file)

New Additions
* Vac Fill Trap - Fills all empty slots in the Vacpack with Pink Slimes
* Vac Spew Trap - Shoots out your entire inventory from your Vacpack. Physics will make some items bounce around as well as they collide when they leave the Vacpack all at once

Balance Adjustments
* Moved Weather Change items from `trap` to `filler`

_I realized that severe weather isn't ever a bad thing since it gives the player rare crafting materials and slimes for the Slimepedia checks. This release moves it to be more prevalent as its in filler category and guarantees that there will be some in every run_

Bug Fixes
* Made a change to when items are reapplied to the save file

_This should hopefully address the reports of some inconsistencies with upgrades being reapplied when loading into the game_

Other
* Changed the item ID ranges for Filler and Traps

_This is what makes it backwards incompatible - some item IDs won't match anymore after this if you're using an older version of the apworld or the mod_
v0.6.0.title: v0.6.0

v0.7.0.description:
## "The Logic Update"

#### Balance Adjustments
- Adds Jetpack-related logic to all zones

_Now games Shouldn't™ have blocked locations because of which sphere the Jetpack is placed in during generation_

#### Quality of Life
- Friendly names added for all Treasure Pods and Shadow Plort Doors

_Starlight Strand Treasure Pod #33? No, that's now "Treasure Pod: Starlight Strand - Top of large blue island" - we now have starter names for what each location is!_

#### Other changes
- Removed Treasure Pod locations Ember Valley #3 and Powderfall Bluffs #24

_It seems like these might be debug locations or something in the game files but I can't seem to figure out how they spawn and they're not on any community-made interactive maps. Now they're gone until someone can prove to me that they should exist again_
v0.7.0.title: v0.7.0

v0.7.1.description:
### Bug Fixes
- Addresses the name overlap on switches between `Rainbow Fields > Ember Valley` and within the Grey Labyrinth from `Waterworks > Impossible Sky`

_This was causing some unintended blocks if the player didn't have Ember Valley access_

### Quality of Life
- Fixed the Archipelago icon in the menus after first visit
- Changed the font in the connection menu so it shows uppercase and lowercase letters
- Added a new warning modal when trying to start a new game without being connected to an Archipelago server
v0.7.1.title: v0.7.1

v0.8.0.description: ### Bug Fixes - Fixes the issue where Ghostly Drones aren't spawning in when they're randomized - Adds the Drone Station Module items into the pool
v0.8.0.title: v0.8.0

v0.9.0.description:
### New Features
- New setting for changing the amount of food to pop a Gordo Slime
  - Allows for percentages from 10-200%
- New setting for adding First Plort Market sale check locations to the pool
  - This setting respects your goal choice and does not include anything from the Grey Labyrinth if you have your goal set as `labyrinth_open`
- New setting for randomizing Ranch Expansions and their locations to the pool
  - Pairs well with `randomize_research_drones` as there's new logic for the ones in the Conservatory
- New setting for toggling whether or not the Fabricator is randomized
- Officially launched support for Plort Door locations
  - Doors still open on this setting, just used for adding locations to the pool

_This rounds out almost all of the intended game options prior to the 1.0 release. There are a couple more that I'm looking into, but we're almost there_

### Goal Changes
- Combined the `prismacore_enter` and `prismacore_stabilize` goals into a single `prismacore` goal

_Not sure if anyone was using both goals since once you can get into the Grey Labyrinth it's just some platforming to get to the Prismacore... If it ends up being requested, I'll bring back `prismacore_enter` in a future update but now the `prismacore` goal is specifically to stabilize it_

### Quality of Life
- Added friendly names to Research Drones
- Added friendly names to Shadow Plort Collector locations
- Disabled `Tarr Spawn`, `Tarr Rain`, and `Teleport` traps once the goal has been met

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed BOb intro conversation logic so it requires access to Ember Valley and Starlight Strand
- Fixed Gigi conversation logic to be correctly tied to the Grey Labyrinth
- Fixed the Powderfall Bluffs entrance Plort Door to count as the Region Gate when region access is randomized
- Fixed the Prismacore fight not being able to start if the player had the Water Tank upgrade but had not crafted the Water Tank spot in the Fabricator
- Fixed the handling for large amounts of incoming items so the game won't freeze
- Fixed jetpack logic for Research Drones and Ghostly Drones
- Fixed the Grey Labyrinth logic rules so that it requires both Ember Valley and Starlight Strand access in order to craft additional Radiant Projectors

### Other Changes
- Updated item IDs with additional buffer space in case future game updates bring in more things
- Updated Slimepedia randomization to be on by default
v0.9.0.title: v0.9.0

v0.9.1.description:
## Quality of Life
* Added friendly names for all Ghostly Drones

## Game Updates
* Added Grey Labyrinth access expectations to the `newbucks` goal

## Bug Fixes
* Fixes additional Jetpack logic in Rainbow Fields
* Fixes ranch expansion logic
* Fixes the in-game Options menu not cycling as expected after viewing the custom Archipelago tab
v0.9.1.title: v0.9.1
